core_content.markdown:
## 3 Generating Functions 

$$\begin{aligned}f^{\prime}(x)&=\sum_{n\geq1}na_{n}x^{n-1}=a_{1}+\sum_{n\geq2}na_{n}x^{n-1}\\&=a_{1}+\sum_{n\geq2}(-a_{n-1}+2na_{n-2})x^{n-1}\\&=a_{1}-\sum_{n\geq2}a_{n-1}x^{n-1}+2x\sum_{n\geq2}na_{n-2}x^{n-2}\\&=a_{1}-(f(x)-a_{0})+2x\left(\sum_{n\geq2}(n-2)a_{n-2}x^{n-2}+2\sum_{n\geq2}a_{n-2}x^{n-2}\right)\\&=a_{1}+a_{0}-f(x)+2x(f^{\prime}(x)+2f(x)).\\ \end{aligned}$$

However, since $a_{1}+a_{0}=0$ ,we get $f^{\prime}(x)=(4x-1)f(x)+2x f^{\prime}(x)$ or, yet,

$$(2x-1)f^{\prime}(x)=-(4x-1)f(x).$$

In order to integrate (i.e, to find the solutionsof)the above diferential equation,note first that $f$ is positive in some interval $(-r,r)$ ,for some $\textstyle0<r\leq{\frac{1}{2}}$ (this comes from the fact that $f(0)=a_{0}=1>0$ and f , being dfferentiable, is continuous, hence has the same sign as $f(0)$ in a suitable neighborhood of 0).Thus, for $|x|<r$ we can write 



$$\frac{f^{\prime}(x)}{f(x)}=-\frac{4x-1}{2x-1}=-2-\frac{1}{2x-1}$$

and then, for $\textstyle|x|<r\leq{\frac{1}{2}}$ 6

$$\begin{aligned}\log f(x)&=\log f(t)\Big|_{0}^{x}=\int_{0}^{x}\frac{f^{\prime}(t)}{f(t)}dt\\&=-\int_{0}^{x}\left(2+\frac{1}{2t-1}\right)dt\\&=-2x-\frac{1}{2}\log(1-2x).\end{aligned}$$

Hence, for $\textstyle|x|<r\leq{\frac{1}{2}}$ we have 

$$f(x)=e^{-2x}(1-2x)^{-1/2}.$$

Step II :firstly, recall that the power series expansion of $e^{-2x}$ is given by letting $a=-2\:\mathrm{i n}\:(3.8)$ , and is valid in the whole real line:

$$e^{-2x}=\sum_{k\geq0}{\frac{(-2)^{k}}{k!}}x^{k}.$$
